title: Christ liveth in me
firstLine: Once far from God and dead in sin,
publicDomainBasis: Daniel Webster Whittle (1840–1901), James McGranahan (1840–1907). All public domain.

sections.label: Verse 1
sections.text:
Once far from God and dead in sin,
No light my heart could see;
But in God’s Word the light I found,
Now Christ liveth in me.

sections.label: Chorus
sections.text:
As rays of light from yonder sun,
The flow’rs of earth set free,
So life and light and love come forth
From Christ living in me.

sections.label: Chorus
sections.text:
As lives the flow’r within the seed,
As in the cone the tree,
So, praise the Christ of truth and grace,
His Spirit dwelleth in me.

sections.label: Chorus
sections.text:
With longing all my heart is filled,
That like Him I may be,
As on the wondrous thought I dwell
That Christ liveth in me.
